automotive-ev-battery.title: CATL is building more than 200 battery swap stations EVERY MONTH
automotive-ev-battery.summary: CATL-backed Choco-SEB battery swap network deployed its 2,000th swapping station on June 30th, demonstrating unprecedented growth in battery exchange infrastructure. The network has averaged over 200 new station deployments monthly throughout 2026, representing a significant expansion of alternative EV charging solutions in Asia.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Beats Tesla — Again
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD has surpassed Tesla in battery electric vehicle sales for another quarter, continuing its dominance in the global EV market. The result underscores the intensifying competition between Chinese and Western automakers in the rapidly growing electric vehicle sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The Hyundai IONIQ 5 remains a top-selling EV as sales cross 20,000 in the first half of 2026
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai's IONIQ 5 crossed 20,000 units sold in the first half of 2026, establishing itself as one of the most popular electric vehicles in the US market. The model's sustained sales performance demonstrates strong consumer demand for mid-range EVs with practical features and pricing.

automotive-ev-battery.title: GM is flooding dealers with Bolt EVs – is that part of the plan?
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors dealers are holding excessive inventory of the resurrected 2027 Chevrolet Bolt EV, with 118 days of supply far exceeding the industry-standard 60-day threshold. The inventory buildup raises questions about whether GM overestimated demand for the affordable electric vehicle despite its single-year production window.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hyundai expands Preferred Partner Network across Europe
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai is expanding its Charge myHyundai charging service's Preferred Partner Network to nine additional European markets while introducing a new Ionity Pro package with one month of free charging for new customers. The expansion reflects ongoing efforts to strengthen EV charging infrastructure across Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric School Buses Put Batteries To Work To Stabilize Grids
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Electric school bus batteries are emerging as ideal candidates for vehicle-to-grid technology to help stabilize electrical grids during peak demand periods. The application demonstrates how EV battery capacity can provide grid services and backup power, creating additional value from school fleet electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Renault introduces Google Gemini via OTA update
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Renault is rolling out Google Gemini AI assistant to vehicles with OpenR Link infotainment systems via over-the-air update, replacing Google Assistant with enhanced natural voice interaction capabilities. The update expands the range of vehicle functions controllable by voice, improving the infotainment experience for customers.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volkswagen's next electric SUV is almost here, and it's not just a rebadged ID.4 [Images]
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volkswagen is preparing to debut the ID. Tiguan electric SUV later this year with significant design and engineering distinctions from the existing ID.4. The new model represents Volkswagen's continued expansion of its electric SUV lineup with differentiated offerings.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The DC metro area is getting its first en-route electric bus chargers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Washington, DC metropolitan area is deploying its first overhead pantograph en-route electric bus chargers, enabling faster charging for transit buses during service operations. This infrastructure development supports the expansion of electric public transportation in the region.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kalmar is building – and selling! – these massive electric reach stackers in China
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kalmar is successfully manufacturing and selling its 45-ton ERG450 electric reach stackers at a Shanghai plant, securing four new orders to close out the second quarter. The commercial success of the heavy-duty cargo handling equipment demonstrates growing demand for electrified industrial equipment in Asian markets.

finance-markets.title: Germany's Second Chance for Growth Rebound Starts Now
finance-markets.summary: German economic data expected this week will reflect the cumulative impact of the Iran war and provide critical signals for Europe's largest economy's growth prospects. The government is preparing its latest policy initiatives to stimulate economic activity amid broader European market concerns.

finance-markets.title: Brazil's Durigan Says Credit Lines Won't Affect Monetary Policy
finance-markets.summary: Brazil's Finance Minister Dario Durigan stated that credit measures introduced by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's administration will not undermine the country's monetary policy framework. The statement addresses investor concerns about potential conflicts between fiscal and monetary policy objectives.

finance-markets.title: First known congressional SpaceX stock buys surface after record IPO
finance-markets.summary: Congressional members have begun purchasing SpaceX stock following the company's record initial public offering, highlighting the deepening commercial ties between the aerospace firm and federal government contracting. The purchases underscore SpaceX's strategic positioning within the current Washington administration.

finance-markets.title: Storms Prompt Evacuations at Washington's July 4 Festivities
finance-markets.summary: Severe thunderstorms are disrupting July 4 celebrations across the eastern United States as a waning heat dome continues to strain regional electricity grids following record power demand. The weather event highlights ongoing energy infrastructure challenges amid extreme heat conditions.

finance-markets.title: Coney Island Boardwalk Set for $1 Billion Upgrade
finance-markets.summary: Coney Island is launching a new growth phase with the establishment of a permanent business improvement district and a planned $1 billion investment to rebuild the boardwalk and support local enterprises. The project aims to strengthen the iconic destination as a year-round community while maintaining its character as a public destination.

finance-markets.title: Venezuela's Official Death Toll From Quake Rises to Almost 3,000
finance-markets.summary: Venezuela's official death toll from last week's twin earthquakes has risen to nearly 3,000, reflecting a significant humanitarian and economic crisis for the South American nation. The escalating casualty figures underscore the scale of damage to critical infrastructure and social systems.

finance-markets.title: Storms force evacuation of July 4 celebrations in Washington
finance-markets.summary: Extreme heat and thunderstorms have forced evacuations at July 4 festivities in Washington DC, disrupting the nation's 250th anniversary celebrations scheduled to include remarks by President Trump at the Lincoln Memorial. The severe weather underscores ongoing climate and infrastructure pressures on major federal events.

finance-markets.title: Iran mourners demand revenge at ayatollah's funeral
finance-markets.summary: A week-long funeral observance for Iran's ayatollah has become a platform for public demands of retaliation, with the regime utilizing the memorial to demonstrate domestic political support. The events reflect ongoing geopolitical tensions that are affecting global markets and economic forecasts.

finance-markets.title: Evacuation ordered at National Mall as storms gather ahead of Trump's America 250 speech
finance-markets.summary: National Guard troops ordered evacuations at the National Mall as severe storms approached ahead of President Trump's scheduled remarks for the nation's 250th anniversary commemoration. The weather disruption affected a major political and ceremonial event on the federal government's calendar.

finance-markets.title: Yankee pyramids: how to build a presidential library
finance-markets.summary: Modern U.S. presidential libraries serve as permanent institutional memorials to each chief executive's tenure and reflect the nation's evolving approach to historical preservation. The architectural and cultural significance of these institutions illuminates broader questions about America's institutional memory and national identity.

tech-ai.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
tech-ai.summary: Midjourney is seeking to compel three Hollywood studios to disclose their own AI usage as part of an ongoing legal dispute. The move highlights growing tensions over AI transparency and usage practices within the entertainment industry.

tech-ai.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
tech-ai.summary: Mistral AI, a French startup founded in 2023, has secured substantial funding to develop open-source AI models with the goal of democratizing frontier artificial intelligence. The company represents a significant competitor to OpenAI in the rapidly expanding generative AI landscape.

tech-ai.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
tech-ai.summary: Alibaba has classified Anthropic's Claude Code as high-risk software and restricted employee access to the tool. The restriction reflects growing corporate concerns about data security and IP protection in relation to AI coding assistants.

tech-ai.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
tech-ai.summary: Fanfiction communities are implementing detection methods to identify and exclude authors using generative AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT. The movement raises concerns about the reliability of AI detection and the potential for false accusations within creative communities.

tech-ai.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
tech-ai.summary: Google has released a commercial exploring a hypothetical scenario in which the Founding Fathers had access to Google Workspace and AI tools. The advertisement reflects ongoing cultural debates about AI's role in creative and intellectual work.

tech-ai.title: NASA launched an emergency mission to stop the Swift Observatory from crashing to Earth
tech-ai.summary: NASA has enlisted Katalyst Space Technologies to launch a rescue mission for the Swift Observatory, which has been pushed into a decaying orbit by recent solar storms. The Link spacecraft aims to intercept the satellite and boost it back to operational altitude to prevent its destruction.

tech-ai.title: Matic's robot vacuum is getting a $250 price hike in September
tech-ai.summary: Matic will increase the price of its flagship robot vacuum by $250 in September, citing rising component costs including memory and related hardware. The company cited tenfold increases in certain component costs as justification for the price adjustment.

tech-ai.title: Visiting the stars (and planets, and telescopes) in VR
tech-ai.summary: The Smithsonian has created a virtual reality experience that allows users to explore stars, exoplanets, and astronomical observatories through immersive walkthrough environments. The VR exhibit represents an innovative approach to science education and public engagement with astronomy.

tech-ai.title: Hey number pad lovers, this is a keyboard we can finally agree on
tech-ai.summary: The Epomaker RT98 mechanical keyboard features a modular number pad that can be repositioned to either side of the board, offering a customizable solution for users with varying preferences. The keyboard combines retro aesthetics with modern features including VIA compatibility and a built-in display.

tech-ai.title: Rocket Report: Indian startup nears first launch; SpaceX's millenary milestone
tech-ai.summary: NASA has awarded Rocket Lab three dedicated launch contracts using its Electron rocket, while an Indian aerospace startup approaches its inaugural launch. The developments underscore growth in the commercial space sector and expanding launch service competition.

science-space.title: NASA's Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms' Length
science-space.summary: New measurements from NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory suggest that the outer spiral arms of the Milky Way galaxy may extend farther than previously believed. This discovery, based on precise distance measurements to dust clouds, could prompt astronomers to revise their understanding of our home galaxy's structure.

science-space.title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA's Swift Observatory
science-space.summary: A pioneering robotic servicing spacecraft named LINK, developed by Katalyst Space, is scheduled to launch no earlier than July 2 to raise the orbit of NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. This first-of-its-kind mission represents a significant advancement in spacecraft servicing and satellite maintenance capabilities.

science-space.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
science-space.summary: NASA is recruiting research participants for a yearlong simulated deep space mission beginning no earlier than August 2027 at the Johnson Space Center in Houston. Volunteers will experience isolated conditions comparable to those expected during crewed lunar and Martian missions, contributing valuable data for future human exploration.

science-space.title: What's Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
science-space.summary: NASA offers practical observing guidance for July 2026, highlighting a predawn Moon-and-planets conjunction, the return of a notable comet, optimal conditions for viewing the Milky Way, and Saturn's rings at a distinctive angle. These events provide accessible opportunities for both casual and experienced skywatchers.

science-space.title: NASA's Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
science-space.summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured a striking image of blue and white stars shining brilliantly against a backdrop of crimson hydrogen gas. The image showcases the telescope's continued capacity to observe stellar populations in stunning detail.

science-space.title: NASA's Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
science-space.summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured an image of red, white, and blue stars that resembles a glittering sparkler against the darkness of space. The image provides a visually striking astronomical observation timely for Independence Day celebrations.

science-space.title: How to find Uranus this week, the hardest planet I've ever tried to see
science-space.summary: A practical guide for skywatchers seeking to observe Uranus, the seventh planet and historically one of the most challenging to locate visually. The article notes that a nearby Mars passage offers an ideal opportunity for locating this distant ice giant.

science-space.title: Human flight was still 7 years away in 1776. Now, we're headed back to the moon
science-space.summary: This article traces humanity's remarkable progression from being earthbound at America's founding in 1776 to planning imminent lunar missions. The piece underscores the dramatic acceleration of space exploration capabilities over 250 years.

science-space.title: What did the night sky look like on the 1st Independence Day 250 years ago?
science-space.summary: An examination of the celestial events visible from North America on July 4, 1776, describing what Benjamin Franklin and other colonists would have observed in the night sky. The article provides historical context connecting astronomy to the founding era.

science-space.title: America 250: From 1776 to the moon and beyond (A Space.com series)
science-space.summary: Space.com's retrospective series examines American achievements in space exploration since the nation's founding and projects future directions in space activity. The comprehensive review connects historical and contemporary developments in American spaceflight.

world-news.title: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
world-news.summary: Ukraine has struck a major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg, with President Volodymyr Zelensky stating the target is key infrastructure that generates revenue for Russia's war effort. The attack represents a significant escalation in Ukraine's targeting of Russian energy infrastructure.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Huge crowds mourn Khamenei, Trump vows calm during funeral
world-news.summary: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and family members were killed in a US-Israeli airstrike in February, and the nation is now observing his funeral with massive public mourning. Donald Trump has pledged to maintain calm during the funeral period amid regional tensions.

world-news.title: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
world-news.summary: Keiko Fujimori, daughter of disgraced former president Alberto Fujimori, has been declared the winner of Peru's presidential election nearly a month after voting took place following a closely contested race. The delayed result determination reflects ongoing tensions in Peru's political landscape.

world-news.title: Lebanon's Aoun calls on US to keep 'always standing beside' his country
world-news.summary: Lebanon's president has issued a statement on US Independence Day urging the United States to maintain its support and partnership with Lebanon as the country seeks to open new diplomatic pathways. The message reflects Lebanon's strategic interest in sustaining US engagement amid regional instability.

world-news.title: Albanians take to the streets in protest for the 35th night in a row
world-news.summary: Massive demonstrations continue in Albania's capital for the 35th consecutive night, with protesters demanding Prime Minister Edi Rama's resignation, government replacement, and constitutional reforms over a proposed luxury construction project. The prolonged unrest signals significant domestic political instability in the Balkan nation.

world-news.title: Pope urges Europe to do more for migrants as he visits gateway island
world-news.summary: Pope Francis has visited the Italian island of Lampedusa, a major entry point for Mediterranean migrants, urging European nations to strengthen their commitment to humanitarian aid and migrant protection. The pontiff paid tribute at a cemetery honoring migrants who perished at sea.

world-news.title: US influence challenged by changing global order
world-news.summary: Analysis by geopolitical expert Paolo von Schirach examines how shifting international dynamics are challenging traditional American geopolitical influence across multiple regions. The assessment reflects broader discussions among policymakers about recalibrating US strategy in a multipolar world.

world-news.title: Brutal heat cancels Fourth of July events, from DC to Philadelphia
world-news.summary: More than 165 million people across the eastern United States are experiencing dangerous heat and humidity conditions, with temperatures expected to remain near 38°C (100°F) for several days, forcing cancellations of major Independence Day celebrations. The extreme weather event highlights the escalating impacts of climate patterns on public life.

world-news.title: Row after Indian state drops eggs from school lunch menu
world-news.summary: India's West Bengal state has sparked a nationwide debate over nutrition policy by removing eggs from school meal programs, raising questions about food choice and nutritional standards in public education. The decision has drawn responses from health advocates and policymakers across the country.

world-news.title: This immigrant served in the US military. Now he faces deportation
world-news.summary: An immigrant who completed three military tours during the Iraq War now faces removal proceedings from the United States to Mexico, raising questions about immigration policy and veterans' protections. The case highlights broader tensions in US immigration enforcement and military service eligibility.

startups-vc.title: Bending Spoons defies SaaS slump, surges 40% on first day of trading
startups-vc.summary: Bending Spoons achieved a strong 40% surge on its first day of public trading, defying the broader SaaS sector slowdown. The company has demonstrated a successful growth strategy by acquiring and revamping legacy technology brands including AOL, Eventbrite, Evernote, Meetup, and Vimeo.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Meesho, a Y Combinator S16 graduate, has completed its initial public offering, establishing itself as one of India's largest e-commerce platforms. The company has built a significant business empowering millions of small merchants across India.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to Groww
startups-vc.summary: Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, has gone public as Y Combinator's first company to list in India. The platform serves tens of millions of users for stock, mutual fund, and ETF investments.

startups-vc.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
startups-vc.summary: Mistral AI, founded in 2023, has emerged as a significant OpenAI competitor by offering open-source AI models with a mission to democratize frontier AI. The company has secured substantial funding since its inception.

startups-vc.title: London-Based Tapestry VC Closes On $80M Third Fund To Invest In Repeat European Founders
startups-vc.summary: Tapestry VC has successfully closed an $80 million third fund focused on repeat founders across Europe. The firm anticipates that the coming wave of AI company exits will generate a new generation of experienced European founders.

startups-vc.title: Autonomous vehicle hype is back, and Humble Robotics is bringing it to freight
startups-vc.summary: The autonomous vehicle sector is experiencing renewed momentum reminiscent of the 2016 hype cycle, with significant capital and talent influx. High-profile entrepreneurs, including Travis Kalanick, are launching new robotics ventures targeting the freight industry.

startups-vc.title: Christopher Golda and Grey Baker Join YC as General Partners
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has appointed Christopher Golda and Grey Baker as General Partners, expanding its investment leadership team. This move strengthens YC's capacity to mentor and fund early-stage startups.

startups-vc.title: Diana Hu Is YC's Newest Managing Partner
startups-vc.summary: Diana Hu has been promoted to Managing Partner at Y Combinator, bringing unique experience of building a startup from inception and shipping technology to 100 million users. This appointment reflects YC's commitment to leadership with proven operational expertise.

startups-vc.title: The Crunchbase Tech Layoffs Tracker
startups-vc.summary: Over 127,000 workers at U.S.-based tech companies experienced mass layoffs in 2025, with layoffs continuing into 2026. The tracker provides ongoing visibility into major workforce reductions across the technology sector.

startups-vc.title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has partnered with nearly two dozen companies to offer students over $25,000 in free AI development tool credits. This initiative aims to reduce financial barriers for founders building AI-enabled startups.

crypto-web3.title: How ethical hackers with just a $3,000 server found a flaw that could've put $70 billion in crypto at risk
crypto-web3.summary: Researchers discovered a critical security vulnerability in the Aptos blockchain that could have enabled attackers to compromise core security guarantees with minimal resources, achieving a near-90% success rate at attack costs of just hundreds of dollars. The flaw was patched before exploitation, but highlights significant risks in blockchain infrastructure security.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin experts split over plan to freeze Satoshi's 1.1 million bitcoin as quantum threat grows
crypto-web3.summary: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao has proposed freezing Satoshi Nakamoto's approximately 1.1 million bitcoins to prevent potential theft by quantum computers, but the proposal has sparked significant debate within the Bitcoin community about feasibility and implications. The discussion reflects growing concerns about quantum computing threats to cryptocurrency security.

crypto-web3.title: UK's bold new crypto rules promise to unlock global trading, but huge compliance hurdles still threaten the rollout
crypto-web3.summary: The United Kingdom's FCA has introduced a comprehensive crypto regulatory framework designed to facilitate global liquidity and institutional adoption, though implementation challenges may complicate the authorization process for market participants. The framework represents a significant step in establishing clear regulatory standards for the sector.

crypto-web3.title: EU moves to block retail investors from explosive boom of multibillion-dollar prediction markets
crypto-web3.summary: European regulators are implementing restrictions on retail investor access to prediction markets, emphasizing that regulatory compliance depends on a product's actual function as a derivative rather than its commercial labeling. The move aims to protect retail investors from exposure to high-risk instruments in the rapidly growing prediction market sector.

crypto-web3.title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin and altcoin exchange deposits have spiked, indicating higher volatility ahead
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin deposits to exchanges have surged to nearly 49,000 BTC, reaching a rare extreme level that has only occurred four other times this year, signaling potential increased market volatility. The spike in exchange deposits typically precedes heightened price movement as traders position for potential sell-offs.

crypto-web3.title: Trump's crypto token buyers are down $3.8 billion, blockchain data shows
crypto-web3.summary: The TRUMP token has declined 96% from its peak, with 85% of secondary market wallets for related token WLFI underwater, representing massive losses for early investors in the politically-linked cryptocurrency. The collapse reflects broader market challenges and risks associated with celebrity and personality-based digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Aave's new Monad market tops $100 million in deposits two days after launch
crypto-web3.summary: Aave's V4 deployment on the Monad network reached $100 million in deposits within two days of launch, with total V4 deposits across platforms exceeding $250 million, marking a new all-time high. The rapid capital inflows demonstrate strong market demand for Aave's latest protocol iteration.

crypto-web3.title: Tokenization's next use case is personalized portfolios, NYLIM executive says
crypto-web3.summary: An executive at New York-based asset manager NYLIM contends that blockchain tokenization can enable complex portfolio construction currently impossible in traditional finance, opening new opportunities for investment customization. The comments highlight institutional interest in blockchain applications beyond simple digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Samsung, Dunamu say they were listed as OUSD stablecoin consortium members without official consultation
crypto-web3.summary: Major Korean technology and cryptocurrency companies Samsung and Dunamu have reported being listed as members of the OUSD stablecoin consortium without their prior knowledge or official approval. The incident raises concerns about governance transparency and due process in emerging stablecoin initiatives.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin jumps above $63,000, reversing end-June losses
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin recovered above $63,000 during thin July 4 trading, marking its highest level in over a month, with XRP leading broader gains among major cryptocurrencies by climbing 5% in 24-hour trading. The price recovery suggests some stabilization in market sentiment following recent declines.

cybersecurity.title: JadePuffer ransomware used AI agent to automate entire attack
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers have identified what appears to be the first documented case of a ransomware operation conducted entirely by a large language model agent, marking a significant escalation in AI-driven cyber attacks. The JadePuffer campaign demonstrates how threat actors are leveraging autonomous AI systems to orchestrate end-to-end ransomware operations without human intervention.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts including those of the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force were briefly compromised after threat actors exploited Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. Instructions for exploiting this vulnerability circulated on Telegram, highlighting a critical flaw in AI-assisted account recovery systems.

cybersecurity.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 108 Malicious Packages and Extensions in PolinRider Campaign
cybersecurity.summary: North Korean threat actors affiliated with the Contagious Interview campaign have deployed 108 malicious packages and browser extensions across npm, Packagist, Go, and Google Chrome repositories as part of the ongoing PolinRider operation. The campaign remains active with attackers continuing to compromise maintainer accounts to distribute additional malicious code.

cybersecurity.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors who gained initial access to thousands of Fortinet firewalls through the FortiBleed vulnerability are now collaborating with ransomware gangs to monetize their foothold. The attackers are simultaneously exploiting a Nextcloud zero-day vulnerability to expand their attack surface.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group 'The Gentlemen?'
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware group has rapidly emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, leveraging an aggressive recruitment strategy that offers affiliates 90 percent of ransom payments. Security researchers have identified clues that may reveal the real-world identity of the group's administrator.

cybersecurity.title: NetNut proxy network disrupted, 2 million infected devices cut off
cybersecurity.summary: A joint operation between authorities and Google has successfully disrupted the NetNut residential proxy network, which provided attackers access to approximately 2 million compromised Android devices including smart TVs and streaming boxes. The takedown represents a significant blow to cybercriminals leveraging the botnet for malicious activities.

cybersecurity.title: Armored Likho Targets Government Agencies, Power Sector with BusySnake Stealer
cybersecurity.summary: A previously undocumented threat actor known as Armored Likho has targeted government agencies and electric power infrastructure across Russia, Brazil, and Kazakhstan using the BusySnake stealer malware. The group combines financially motivated attacks against individuals with targeted cyber espionage operations against critical infrastructure organizations.

cybersecurity.title: 'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat
cybersecurity.summary: Researchers have identified a novel attack vector called phantom squatting, in which large language models hallucinate web domains for legitimate brands that threat actors then register for malicious purposes. This AI-driven supply chain attack is difficult to detect and represents an emerging threat to brand security and customer trust.

cybersecurity.title: U.S. Government Entity Paid Kairos $1 Million in Data-Theft Extortion Case
cybersecurity.summary: A U.S. government entity paid approximately $1 million to a group calling itself Kairos to prevent stolen data from being publicly released, according to analysis of leaked negotiation transcripts and blockchain payment records. Notably, researchers found no evidence that Kairos is actually a ransomware gang or ever deployed encryption, suggesting it may operate solely as a data extortion operation.

cybersecurity.title: ARToken PhaaS exposes EvilTokens' Microsoft 365 phishing toolkit
cybersecurity.summary: The ARToken phishing-as-a-service platform has been discovered operating as an affiliate of the EvilTokens platform, providing researchers insight into an extensive toolkit designed to compromise Microsoft 365 accounts. The PhaaS platform represents the ongoing evolution of commoditized phishing infrastructure available to threat actors.

health-biotech.title: U.S. judge blocks Colorado's first-of-its-kind price cap on an Amgen drug
health-biotech.summary: A federal judge granted Amgen's preliminary injunction to block Colorado's attempt to implement a 70% price cap on Enbrel, a blockbuster autoimmune drug, citing potential economic injury to the company. This decision represents a significant legal setback for state-level drug price regulation efforts and has broad implications for pharmaceutical pricing policy across the United States.

health-biotech.title: Roche drug sets new standard for KRAS-driven lung cancer
health-biotech.summary: Roche's new treatment has demonstrated efficacy in KRAS-driven lung cancer, establishing a novel standard of care for this challenging patient population. This development represents a significant clinical advance in precision oncology and expands therapeutic options for lung cancer patients with this specific genetic mutation.

health-biotech.title: Halfway into the year, biotech is booming
health-biotech.summary: The biotech sector has experienced sustained momentum throughout 2026, with stock rallies that began in April continuing to accelerate through mid-year. This market strength reflects robust investor confidence in the sector and suggests favorable conditions for biotech companies and capital formation in the industry.

health-biotech.title: Medicare takes another swing at 340B cuts to hospitals
health-biotech.summary: Medicare has proposed additional reductions to payments for drugs purchased under the 340B drug discount program, continuing the government's efforts to address costs associated with hospital drug purchases. This latest proposal in the ongoing debate over 340B drug discounts reflects mounting pressure to control pharmaceutical spending within the Medicare program.

health-biotech.title: Lawmakers urge HHS to force Eli Lilly to provide 340B drug discounts to hospitals
health-biotech.summary: Members of Congress have called on the Department of Health and Human Services to compel Eli Lilly to participate in the 340B drug discount program for hospitals. This legislative intervention highlights the ongoing tension between pharmaceutical manufacturers and healthcare institutions over drug pricing and discount program participation.

health-biotech.title: Viridian may have edge over Amgen in eye drug showdown, analysts argue
health-biotech.summary: Following FDA approval, Viridian's thyroid eye disease drug has received a product label that positions it competitively against Amgen's Tepezza, according to Wall Street analysts. The regulatory decision provides Viridian with potential market advantages in the emerging thyroid eye disease treatment space.

health-biotech.title: Ipsen's $450M blood cancer drug; FDA selects first cohort for 'pre-check' pilot program
health-biotech.summary: Ipsen has announced plans to acquire Kartos Therapeutics, securing a late-stage asset for myelofibrosis treatment in a deal valued at $450 million, while the FDA has launched a new pre-check pilot program aimed at streamlining drug development. These developments reflect ongoing consolidation in oncology and regulatory innovation to accelerate drug approvals.

health-biotech.title: These biotechs just landed big fundraising hauls. Here's what they pitched to investors.
health-biotech.summary: Biotech companies focused on eye diseases, autoimmune conditions, and longevity medicine have secured substantial funding rounds, demonstrating strong investor appetite for these therapeutic areas. The capital raises underscore investor confidence in emerging treatment modalities and unmet medical needs within these disease categories.

health-biotech.title: Elevance sues government over $115 million tied to Medicare Advantage star ratings
health-biotech.summary: Health insurer Elevance has filed suit against the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, alleging improper calculation of its star ratings that affected $115 million in payments. The legal action raises questions about CMS's methodology for rating health plans and payment determination processes.

health-biotech.title: 5 FDA decisions to watch in the third quarter of 2026
health-biotech.summary: Several important FDA approvals are anticipated in the third quarter of 2026 that may provide insight into the agency's regulatory flexibility under new leadership. These decisions will likely influence industry approaches to drug development and regulatory strategy in coming months.

energy-climate.title: PJM anticipates new peak demand record as heat wave tests power grid
energy-climate.summary: PJM Interconnection forecasts Thursday's demand could break its 2006 summer peak record of 165,563 MW as an intense heat wave strains the U.S. power grid. The grid operator has received approval to curtail data centers and other large loads as a last resort to manage the emergency.

energy-climate.title: Cropped 1 July 2026: Heatwave scorches Europe | UK 2050 farm plan | What's next for the High Seas Treaty
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's curated digest highlights major climate developments including an unprecedented heatwave affecting Europe, the UK's new agricultural strategy through 2050, and ongoing negotiations for the High Seas Treaty. The compilation addresses critical intersections of climate change, land use, and food security.

energy-climate.title: Analysts expect rising PPA prices as clean energy tax credits phase out
energy-climate.summary: Clean energy project developers anticipate significantly higher power purchase agreement (PPA) prices as federal tax credits diminish, with analysts noting that lost incentives must be recovered through increased pricing. The shift could impact renewable energy project economics and deployment rates across the United States.

energy-climate.title: Guest post: Climate change has caused one-fifth of Pine Island glacier retreat
energy-climate.summary: New research quantifies that climate change is responsible for approximately 20% of the rapid retreat observed at Pine Island glacier, one of West Antarctica's fastest-changing ice masses. The findings underscore the direct link between anthropogenic warming and accelerating ice sheet destabilization.

energy-climate.title: Why the true cost of new gas plants is much higher than the sticker price
energy-climate.summary: GridLab analysis reveals that regulatory reviews of proposed gas-fired power plants frequently underestimate total project costs by failing to account for pipeline, fuel, and storage infrastructure expenses, which can add approximately 30% to a project's overall cost. The findings highlight a critical accounting gap in utility planning and cost-benefit analyses.

energy-climate.title: New Jersey lawmakers send data center tariff bill to governor
energy-climate.summary: New Jersey legislators have passed legislation imposing tariffs on new and existing data centers exceeding 50 MW capacity to insulate ratepayers from costs associated with the artificial intelligence data boom. The measure addresses grid impacts and cost allocation concerns amid rapid data center expansion.

energy-climate.title: Blocking, El Niño and extreme events redistribute solar resource in 2026 so far
energy-climate.summary: Solar resource data from Solcast reveals that shifting atmospheric patterns—including early-year blocking, polar vortex disruption, and emerging El Niño conditions—have created above-average solar irradiance across Europe, the United States, and select regions of Asia and Africa in 2026's first half. Conditions in other regions including Canada and parts of Russia have been more variable.

energy-climate.title: DOE wants to 'permanently end' appliance efficiency requirements
energy-climate.summary: The Department of Energy has proposed a new rule that would restrict the appliance efficiency standards program with significant procedural obstacles, which consumer advocates argue would undermine decades of energy conservation policy. The proposal threatens to derail ongoing efforts to reduce residential and commercial energy consumption.

energy-climate.title: Agrivoltaic cold frame system for controlled-environment crop cultivation
energy-climate.summary: Researchers have developed an innovative agrivoltaic system integrating semi-transparent photovoltaic modules to establish controlled microclimates for crop production while generating on-site renewable electricity. Canadian field trials demonstrated substantial lettuce yield improvements, demonstrating the dual-purpose potential of agricultural-solar land integration.

energy-climate.title: EPA's tougher soot standard upheld in court
energy-climate.summary: The D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals rejected legal challenges from states and industry groups, upholding the EPA's stricter National Ambient Air Quality Standards for fine particulate matter emissions from power plants and other sources. The decision maintains the strengthened air quality regulations despite ongoing litigation over implementation details.

asia-pacific.title: Why US and China are struggling to reach a consensus over future shape of relationship
asia-pacific.summary: The United States and China have agreed to establish a "constructive" relationship based on "strategic stability," but significant underlying differences remain unresolved, raising questions about the sustainability of improved bilateral relations. As President Xi Jinping considers a potential US visit later this year, experts note the absence of clear deliverables typically required for such high-level diplomatic engagements.

asia-pacific.title: 'Overcapacity' talk reflects a West irked by China's industrial rise
asia-pacific.summary: Western nations, particularly Europe, are increasingly critical of China's industrial expansion in clean energy sectors such as solar panels, wind turbines, and electric vehicles, despite their stated need for rapid and affordable green energy deployment. The debate over China's "overcapacity" reveals underlying tensions about market dominance and technological competition in the global clean energy transition.

asia-pacific.title: Asean monitors: 'simply theatre' in Thai-Cambodian narrative war?
asia-pacific.summary: ASEAN military observers have visited both Thailand and Cambodia to assess damage and verify ceasefire compliance following deadly clashes in the previous year that resulted in over 100 military casualties. The monitoring effort highlights the ongoing territorial disputes and competing narratives between the two nations regarding the breach of ceasefire agreements.

asia-pacific.title: China offers sea cable detection tech to clients in Middle East, Europe and Asia
asia-pacific.summary: China is exporting advanced robotic technology capable of locating marine cables buried beneath the seabed to Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Europe, positioning itself strategically in the global marine equipment sector. The initiative aims to expand Chinese influence and integration in offshore engineering markets across multiple regions.

asia-pacific.title: Why Hong Kong students are heading to mainland China to intern
asia-pacific.summary: An increasing number of Hong Kong students are pursuing summer internship opportunities in mainland China through expanded government and university schemes, attracted by greater career opportunities, hands-on experience in a larger market, and lower living costs. This trend reflects growing integration between Hong Kong and mainland China's economies and labor markets.

asia-pacific.title: Understanding China's Party-State Intelligence System
asia-pacific.summary: An analysis of China's party-state intelligence apparatus clarifies distinctions in how the system operates, challenging characterizations of "whole of society" intelligence gathering that risk perpetuating discrimination against ethnic Chinese communities. The examination provides nuanced insight into the structure and functioning of China's security apparatus.

asia-pacific.title: Chinese tea chain Molly Tea ordered to pay Louis Vuitton US$1.5 million for trademark infringement
asia-pacific.summary: A Chinese court has ruled against Shenzhen-based Molly Tea, ordering the company to pay Louis Vuitton US$1.5 million in damages for trademark infringement, though the tea chain has announced plans to appeal the decision. The case highlights ongoing intellectual property disputes involving Chinese companies and international luxury brands.

asia-pacific.title: Guam: Where America's 250th Birthday Will First Be Honored
asia-pacific.summary: As the United States celebrates its 250th anniversary, strategic attention is being directed to Guam, a critical outpost for American military and geopolitical interests in the Asia-Pacific region. The article emphasizes Guam's significant but often underrecognized contribution to US security and defense in the region.

asia-pacific.title: Putin and Trump discuss Ukraine, Iran in US Independence Day call
asia-pacific.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin and US President Donald Trump held an 85-minute telephone conversation on US Independence Day, discussing Ukraine negotiations and the upcoming NATO summit scheduled for Turkey on July 7-8. The call represents a significant diplomatic engagement between the two leaders on critical geopolitical issues affecting global security.

asia-pacific.title: Venezuela quake death toll rises to nearly 3,000
asia-pacific.summary: Venezuela's twin earthquakes on June 24 have resulted in nearly 3,000 confirmed deaths, with thousands more missing and homeless in the aftermath of one of Latin America's most devastating seismic disasters. International rescue operations have begun winding down as recovery efforts transition to longer-term humanitarian response.
